message:
test(conformance): differential harness for sys/stat.h vs glibc

CONFORMANCE: libc sys/stat.h diff matrix.

7 functions covered with per-test tempdirs to avoid cross-test
interference:
- stat   → 5 cases: regular file, empty file, large file (4096B),
  directory, missing path. Compares 12 stat fields exhaustively.
- lstat  → 2 cases: regular file vs symlink (asserts lstat sees the
  link itself, not its target).
- fstat  → 2 cases: open fd on regular file, invalid fd 99999
  (return + errno).
- chmod  → 5 modes (0644/0600/0755/0400/0000); creates parallel
  files for FrankenLibC and glibc, applies mode, then verifies via
  libc::stat that st_mode bits match.
- mkdir/rmdir → 2 modes (0755/0700); creates, verifies re-create
  fails with EEXIST identically, then rmdir.
- umask → 4 masks (022/002/077/000); compares the "previous mask"
  return; serialized via UMASK_LOCK because umask is process-global.

Per case we compare return value, errno on failure, and (for stat
family) every meaningful libc::stat field: st_dev/st_ino/st_mode/
st_nlink/st_uid/st_gid/st_rdev/st_size/st_blksize/st_blocks/
st_mtime/st_ctime. atime is intentionally skipped because it can
vary between back-to-back calls.

0 divergences observed. sys/stat.h family is conformant.

Full abi suite: 3023/3023 pass across 63 suites (was 3016/62;
+7 from new harness).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
